description:
## Symptom

A closure-lit Var typed by the typer as `&fnN[caps]:(any) -> any`,
invoked via `TailCallClosure` from a caller whose narrow spec passes
RawInt-typed args, produces wrong runtime values: the callee body
interprets the wire arg as already-tagged when the caller passed it
raw (or vice versa). Wire i64 types match; semantic Tagged/RawInt
contract on slot 0 does not.

## Minimal repro

```fz
fn each(_, []), do: nil
fn each(f, [h | t]) do
  f(h)
  each(f, t)
end

fn main() do
  k = 10
  each(fn(x) -> print(x + k), [1, 2, 3])
end
```

Expected stdout:
```
11
12
13
```

Actual stdout:
```
19
27
35
```

Decode: 19 = 1*8+1 +10, 27 = 2*8+1 +10, 35 = 3*8+1 +10. The list head
`h` arrives at the lambda body wrapped with the int-tag bits already
applied, and the body's RawInt-typed arithmetic adds 10 to the boxed
value rather than the raw value.

## Where the mismatch is emitted

each_s17 lowers `f(h)` as:

```
v18 = load.i64 v0+16            ; body_func_id loaded from closure
return_call_indirect sig4, v18(v11, v0, v16)
```

with `sig4 = (i64, i64, i64) -> i64 tail` (closure-target / indirect
sig — all-i64, convention is Tagged at every arg slot).

The callee is `lambda_16_s22`, narrow spec, ABI `(RawInt, RawInt) ->
Tagged`. Same wire (all i64), incompatible semantics on slot 0. The
body's int-arithmetic reads `v0` as already-raw.

## Why the indirect path was taken

`ir_codegen.rs:4994–5045` has a `lit_resolved` fast path that would
emit a direct `return_call` with the body's narrow sig:

```rust
let lit_resolved: Option<(u32, FuncId, usize)> = (|| {
    let (body_fn_id, body_sid) =
        resolve_tcc_body(closure, args, fn_types, module, spec_registry)?;
    let body_fid = *fn_ids.get(&body_sid)?;
    let n_caps = closure_n_captures.get(&body_fn_id).copied().unwrap_or(0);
    Some((body_sid, body_fid, n_caps))
})();
```

For this repro, lit_resolved returns None even though the typer
narrowed the closure Var to a singleton lit (`fz dump` shows
`v0 :: &fn16[10]:(any) -> any`). The fall-through to indirect is the
ABI-violating path.

Three candidate failure points inside lit_resolved (in order of
suspicion):

1. **`resolve_tcc_body`'s `ft.vars.get(closure)?.as_closure_lit()?`
   returns None.** The `Descr` displays as a singleton lit but
   carries internal shape `as_closure_lit` doesn't recognize —
   non-empty `neg`, multiple `pos` clauses, or `pos[0].lit` is None
   and the arrow is only structural. See `src/types.rs:507`.
2. **`fn_ids.get(&body_sid)` returns None.** The resolved narrow
   spec didn't get an emitted Cranelift FuncId (sentinel slot or
   spec-without-body). With `ensure_any_keys` total this is unlikely
   for reachable fns, but a regression path exists if reachability
   misses an opaque callee.
3. **`closure_n_captures.get(&body_fn_id).unwrap_or(0)` silently
   mis-routes.** Missing entry defaults to 0; the resulting sig has
   the wrong param count; the call would still type-check at the
   Cranelift level but pass the wrong slot. Direct call may still
   succeed structurally with wrong semantics — needs verification.

## Repro context

This shape was unreachable pre-`fz-aiz` arc — the codegen panicked at
`.29.12.1` ('no covering spec for Cont') before reaching the wire-ABI
mismatch. The arc made the annotation-driven dispatch path total
(every reachable FnId has an any-key floor; codegen reads typer-
chosen SpecIds from IR annotations; no resolve calls at dispatch
sites). With the panic gone, programs that touch this shape now
compile and the latent indirect-ABI mismatch is observable.

The 166-fixture matrix doesn't catch it because all closures in the
matrix are either:
- zero-capture + direct dispatch (no lit_resolved seam exercised), or
- fully opaque (spawn target — body emitted with Tagged ABI, no
  mismatch on the wire).

The middle case — singleton closure-lit with non-empty captures called
via TailCallClosure in a non-tail-recursive HOF — was unrepresented.

## Two viable fixes

**(A) Make `lit_resolved` actually fire.** Trace each None-returning
point and either repair the `Descr` shape upstream (typer) or relax
`as_closure_lit`'s gating to accept the wider shapes we display as
singleton lits. Principled fix; aligns runtime behavior with what
the type printer claims. This is the right structural answer.

**(B) Coerce per-arg before the indirect call.** The body's
`param_reprs` are reachable via `fn_ids[body_sid]`/`param_reprs[sid]`
even when `lit_resolved` falls through. Walk the args, coerce each
from caller-repr to the body's narrow-repr at slot i, then indirect-
call with a sig built from those reprs. Defense-in-depth; works
regardless of why lit_resolved is unhappy.

(A) preferred. Land (B) as a safety net if (A) needs significant
typer work.

## Acceptance

Repro program above prints `11 / 12 / 13`. Fixture-matrix stays
green. Add a regression fixture exercising this exact shape (a
recursive HOF over a list, calling a closure-lit with non-empty
captures and non-tail recursion through the HOF), with golden
specs/clif goldens checked in.

## See also

- fz-aiz arc — annotation-driven dispatch (epic that exposed this).
- ir_codegen.rs:4994–5045 — the lit_resolved seam.
- ir_codegen.rs:1806 — resolve_tcc_body.
- types.rs:507 — `as_closure_lit`.

## Quicksort follow-up

This bug also blocks the natural quicksort fixture (with a `filter`
HOF taking `fn(x) -> x < p` closures). Once this is fixed, the
quicksort fixture should land cleanly as an integration test for
the closure-lit / non-tail recursion path.

comments.text:
Root chased.

Symptom: each(fn(x) -> x+k, [1,2,3]) with k=10 prints 19, 27, 35
instead of 11, 12, 13. The wrong values decode as `(h*8+1) + k_raw`,
i.e. the caller passed h TAGGED and the callee added it as RAW.

Where: each_s17's TailCallClosure to the lambda lowers to:

  v18 = load.i64 v0+16
  return_call_indirect sig4, v18(v11, v0, v16)   ; v11 = list head, tagged

sig4 = (i64, i64, i64) -> i64 tail. That's the closure-target / indirect
sig — all-i64 with the convention that arg slots are Tagged. But the
callee `lambda_16_s22` has narrow ABI (RawInt, RawInt) -> Tagged. Wire
types match; semantic contract on slot 0 does not.

Why the indirect path: ir_codegen.rs:4994–5045 has a `lit_resolved`
fast-path that would emit a direct return_call with the body's narrow
sig. It's returning None for this case despite the dump showing
v0 :: &fn16[10]:(any) -> any (a singleton closure_lit).

Three candidate failure points inside lit_resolved (in order of
suspicion):
  1. resolve_tcc_body's `ft.vars.get(closure)?.as_closure_lit()?`
     returns None — Descr carries lit info in a form as_closure_lit
     doesn't recognize (union with another arrow, non-empty neg, etc).
  2. fn_ids.get(&body_sid) returns None — the resolved narrow spec
     didn't get an emitted Cranelift FuncId (sentinel or unreachable).
  3. closure_n_captures.get(&body_fn_id).unwrap_or(0) silently
     mis-routes when the entry is missing — n_caps=0 produces a wrong
     sig length but the path still falls through to indirect.

Bug pre-existed fz-aiz arc. Pre-arc, programs in this shape panicked
at .29.12.1 before reaching the wire-ABI mismatch. The arc removed
the panic; the underlying invariant break is now visible.

Two viable fixes (both outside fz-aiz):

A. Make lit_resolved actually fire. Trace each None-returning point
   and either fix the Descr / spec-registration upstream or relax the
   gating (e.g., as_closure_lit accepting wider shapes).

B. Make the indirect path coerce per-arg from caller-repr to callee-
   narrow-repr. The body's param_reprs are available — peek at them
   via body_func_id and coerce args before the indirect call.

(A) is the principled fix; (B) is a defense-in-depth seam.

title: TailCallClosure ABI mismatch: closure-lit fast path falls through to Tagged-wire indirect for narrow callees
